article_title: FTC and Eight States Settle With WPP, Publicis, and Dentsu Over Alleged Brand Safety Collusion, Ending the Industry's Coordinated Misinformation Floor

content_preview.title: FTC and Eight States Settle With WPP, Publicis, and Dentsu Over Alleged Brand Safety Collusion, Ending the Industry's Coordinated Misinformation Floor
content_preview.summary: The Federal Trade Commission and a coalition of eight states secured a proposed consent order banning the three holding companies from coordinating brand safety standards or maintaining shared exclusion lists.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Three of the world's largest advertising holding companies have agreed to abandon coordinated brand safety standards under a proposed consent order with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and eight state attorneys general. The settlement, announced on April 15, 2026, resolves antitrust allegations that GroupM Worldwide LLC (operating as WPP Media), Publicis, Inc., and Dentsu US, Inc. conspired since 2018 to impose a uniform "Brand Safety Floor" that the FTC says distorted competition...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Both round-1 findings cleanly addressed. Bot took the 'trim rather than expand' approach — removed unsupported specifics rather than chasing additional sources. Clean fix.

round_1_disposition.finding_1_100_member_companies: Bot accepted finding. Removed '100 member companies' and 'August 2024' specifics that weren't in Fox Business. The new GARM-history paragraph cites only what Fox Business actually contains: Loerke 'discontinuing' announcement and 'GARM was a nonprofit with limited resources to defend itself in court' (the 'to defend itself in court' inference is reasonable given the broader Fox Business context of the X+Rumble lawsuit driving the discontinuation).
round_1_disposition.finding_2_christchurch_2019_founding: Bot accepted finding. The 'launched by WFA in 2019 in the wake of the Christchurch attack' sentence was removed entirely. The article doesn't need the GARM origin story to make its case — the FTC source already provides the relevant GARM/APB context.

editor_notes.source_verification: Re-using round-1 verification for the unchanged FTC primary source content (Ferguson quotes, 8 states, Northern District of Texas, GARM/APB framework, 'starting in 2018', etc.) and Fox Business content (X+Rumble suit, defendants list, Yaccarino billions, House Judiciary 'directly organized boycotts' quote, Loerke 'discontinuing'). The new GARM-history paragraph is now strictly within what Fox Business supports.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims now verified. The unsupported '100 member companies' and Christchurch/2019 specifics are gone; what remains is fully supported by the cited snapshots.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. The Analysis section's framing of antitrust-vs-speech is editorial but defensible.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Both round-1 findings cleanly addressed by trimming. Article is now strictly within source-supported claims.
